page: Microsoft Azure FX48-12ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 6 physical cores and 12 virtual CPUs with dedicated allocation, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. A key hardware characteristic is its massive memory capacity of 1008.0 GB, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per physical core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for tile and int8 operations.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities. It achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores, falls within the average range, reflecting the lower physical core count. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches 1036.67 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 102.17 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The primary tradeoff of the FX48-12ms_v2 is its resource asymmetry. While it provides over a terabyte of RAM, it is constrained by having only 12 vCPUs and no local storage or GPU accelerators. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this profile is optimized for workloads that require vast memory capacity but do not scale with high CPU core counts, allowing organizations to avoid paying for unneeded compute cores to obtain high memory limits.

This server is designed for memory-intensive applications. It is highly suitable for large in-memory databases, caching platforms, and enterprise applications with massive working sets that benefit from high single-core performance and extensive RAM.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48-12ms_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server configured with an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs across 6 physical cores and an exceptionally large memory allocation of 1008.0 GB, resulting in 84.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, representing a clear tradeoff that prioritizes memory capacity over raw compute density. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core and text processing performance, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core metrics remain in the average tier. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallelization. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring a massive memory footprint paired with strong single-core execution.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-12ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 12 vCPUs, and 1008 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-12ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 1008 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive server combining a terabyte of RAM with strong single-core performance for specialized database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
Massive 1008.0 GB RAM capacity yielding 84.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for in-memory databases and large caching layers
